summary: High‑octane set with scorching Big Railroad Blues and stellar Althea
blurb: The show bursts with energy from the opening Cassidy through a fiery Big Railroad Blues, then lifts into an inspired Althea. The second set builds to a soaring Space → Wheel sequence capped by a powerful Throwing Stones > Not Fade Away stretch.
review: On August 21, 1983 at Frost Amphitheatre the Dead delivered a tightly wound, high‑octane performance that still feels fresh today. The first set kicks off with a crisp Cassidy and rolls through a blistering Big Railroad Blues before Jerry’s mystical guitar on Althea sends the crowd into a reverent hush. The second half is where the magic truly unfolds – a seamless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue launches into an expansive Playing In The Band, leading into a clean‑cut Drum and Space excursion that morphs into a soaring Wheel. The band caps the night with a propulsive Throwing Stones > Not Fade Away and a heartfelt encore of It’s All Over Now Baby Blue. Across multiple recordings the audience hears Jerry “going completely crazy” on his guitar, Phil’s bass “bombs” out in the double‑encore, Brent’s organ solo adds depth, and Mickey and Billy lock in with a crisp THX‑style finish. The best source captures all of this with crystal‑clear separation, making it an essential listen for anyone hunting that 1983 spark.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three recordings (audience, soundboard, unknown) are explicitly positive, praising Althea, Big Railroad Blues, and the Wheel; two others are mixed but contain no negative comments about playing. The consistent high praise across multiple sources warrants a moderate positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent audience tape with crystal‑clear separation, no major dropouts, and captures the full high‑octane energy of the show.

key_highlights: Scorching Big Railroad Blues that drives the first set.
Transcendent Althea moment with Jerry’s spoken line and mystical guitar tones.
Seamless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue into a powerful Wheel stretch.
Tight rhythm section—Phil’s “bombs” on bass and solid drumming from Mickey & Billy.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia goes completely crazy with mystical guitar tones, crunchy reverb and an upfront presence; extra effort shines on Althea and the high‑energy jams.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh’s bass sits high, tight and powerful—especially notable during the double encore where his “bombs” drive the low end.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard work adds depth throughout; an organ solo (Rooster) is highlighted as dynamic and great.
band_performance.Mickey: Provides solid percussive support with a clean THX‑style ending, helping to anchor the high‑octane sections.
band_performance.Billy: Locks in with Mickey, delivering tight drumming that propels the second set’s jams.
